allowed_assumptions: Input is not modified externally during processing.
constraints: Each vector's L2 norm must be computed exactly once.
Zero vectors must not cause division by zero.
disallowed_assumptions: All vectors have the same length.
Norm can be recomputed freely (no caching needed).
expected_failure_modes: Computing the norm of the same vector multiple times.
Division by zero for zero vectors.
prohibited_claims: Model superiority — not authorized by this task.
Wrapper improvement without paired comparison — not measured.
prompt:
Implement `normalize_vectors(vectors: list[list[float]]) -> list[list[float]]`.

Normalize each vector to unit length (L2 norm).

Constraints:
- Compute each vector's norm exactly once; do not recompute.
- Do not create unnecessary intermediate lists.
- Use a single pass per vector where possible.

Handle:
- Zero vectors (all zeros) — return the zero vector unchanged.
- Empty vectors — return empty vector.
- Empty list of vectors — return [].
- Vectors with different lengths — normalize each independently.
- Negative values in vectors.
title: Batch Normalization Without Unnecessary Loops
